title: Neznámý titulek
content: Elite BJJ competitors Marcus ‘Buchecha’ Almeida and Max Gimenis will now be fighting each other in the next step of their respective MMA careers. It’s a somewhat unusual choice by the UFC, as the two men have already shared the mats twice before and both of those matches ended with Buchecha winning by submission. Obviously things can go a lot differently when there’s strikes involved but neither of them have shown a particularly high level of striking so far. Gimenis has gotten more use out of his striking than Buchecha so far, but he’s also suffered a pair of knockout losses in the process. They actually have pretty similar records as far as MMA goes, as Gimenis is 6-2 after losing his UFC debut last year while Buchecha is 5-2-1 and still winless after two fights in the octagon. Their BJJ careers were worlds apart though, with Buchecha winning more IBJJF World Championship titles than anyone else before and leaving at the very top of the sport. Gimenis stood on the podium there a few times in gi and no gi, and now he gets a chance to test himself against Buchecha in the cage. They will be facing each other at UFC Vegas 116 on May 25th, 2026 and they aren’t the only BJJ competitors on the card either. Rodolfo Vieira will also be making an appearance against Eric McConico, as he looks to bounce back from a loss and continue his climb up the middleweight rankings. He’s had mixed results in the octagon, with a 6-4 promotional record against some of the best in his weight class. Shortly after him, Talita Alencar will be stepping into the cage too and she has the most UFC success out of any of them. She’s 3-1 in the UFC and 7-1-1 overall, and this fight against Julia Polastri is a great chance for her to break into the rankings for the first time ever. Tags: Marcus 'Buchecha' AlmeidaMax GimenisUFC
